figures.caption: Figure 1: Overview of missing-modality learning paradigms and the proposed FEDMPO.

Figure 5: Ablation study of FEDMPO. (a) Consistent performance drops in variants (w/o AGMG, MoE, Align) prove the necessity of each structural design. (b) Comparisons w/ and w/o RelAgg confirm that reliability-aware aggregation is critical for stabilizing performance and reducing training costs.
Table 1: Performance comparison of our proposed method against baselines across Node Classification, Link Prediction, and Modality Retrieval tasks.

Figure 3: Ablation study of FEDMPO. We compare the full model with variants removing AGMG, MoE fusion, cross-modal alignment, and reliability-aware aggregation across three downstream tasks. The performance drops verify the effectiveness of each component.
Figure 2: The overview of the proposed FEDMPO.
Figure 6: System efficiency comparison across graph federated learning baselines. FedMPO achieves high throughput and low memory footprint.

Table 2: Robustness Study on the Ele-fashion dataset. Left: Sensitivity to varying modality missing rates (η). Right: Adaptability to data heterogeneity across clients under different Dirichlet distribution parameters (α).

Figure 4: Sensitivity and robustness analysis of FEDMPO. (a) Node classification ACC on EleFashion and Grocery under different combinations of reconstruction coefficient λrec and routing coefficient λroute. (b) Robustness of FEDMPO under different Dirichlet α values and modality missing rates η.
Table 3: Compute environment used for all experiments.
